data.title: Mining company MARA transferred out another 250 BTC, bringing its total sales in March to 15,133 BTC.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, Bitcoin mining company MARA transferred out another 250 BTC, worth approximately $17.37 million, three hours ago. Previous data showed that the company had sold a total of 15,133 BTC, worth approximately $1.1 billion, between March 4th and March 25th.

data.title: Ethena: Will expand neutral arbitrage trading to stock and commodity markets
data.description: Ethena has officially announced that USDe will expand its collateral types following approval from its independent risk committee. Ethena stated that historically, USDe's collateral primarily consisted of short perpetual contracts against spot crypto assets, but currently, perpetual contract positions only account for 11% of USDe's collateral, with the remainder allocated to various stablecoin reserves and DeFi lending positions. Any portfolio concentrated on a single strategy inherently carries risk. While USDe's past collateral framework has never resulted in damage to collateral, depletion of reserve funds, or critical issues, the project is actively pursuing collateral diversification to reduce concentration risk and build a more resilient reserve portfolio. This diversification of collateral assets encompasses four directions, each a natural extension of Ethena's existing business: 1. Overcollateralized institutional stablecoin lending: custodianing the collateral provided by borrowers through a third-party institutional custodian; 2. More Real-World Assets (RWA) beyond US Treasury Bonds (T-Bills): expanding to more high-quality, highly liquid credit assets; 3. Equity and Commodity Basis Trading: extending Ethena's delta-neutral hedging approach, already applied to crypto assets, to traditional assets; 4. Prime Lending for Trading Firms: providing funding to trading firms through overcollateralized loans, with the counterparty assuming the exchange risk.

data.title: Australia's March TD-MI Inflation Index Rises to 4.3%
data.description: Australia's March TD-MI Inflation Index showed an annual increase of 4.3%, according to Jin10. This marks a rise from the previous value of 3.60%, indicating a notable uptick in inflationary pressures within the country. The data reflects ongoing economic trends and may influence future monetary policy decisions.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold and Silver Prices Decline Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Gold prices have fallen below $4,630 per ounce, marking a daily decrease of 0.45%. According to Jin10, New York silver futures have also experienced a decline, with a daily drop of 1.00%, currently trading at $72.10 per ounce. The market volatility continues to impact precious metals, reflecting broader economic uncertainties.

data.title: Ethena Announces Expansion of USDe Collateral Types
data.description:
Ethena has announced plans to diversify the collateral types for USDe following approval from an independent risk committee. According to BlockBeats, the expansion will cover four key areas, each representing a natural extension of Ethena's existing operations.

The first area involves over-collateralized institutional stablecoin lending, where collateral assets provided by borrowers will be managed by third-party institutional custodians. The second area focuses on real-world assets (RWA) beyond U.S. Treasury Bills, aiming to include more high-quality, liquid credit assets.

Ethena also plans to extend its delta-neutral hedging strategies, already applied to crypto assets, to traditional assets such as stocks and commodities. Lastly, the company will offer prime lending to trading institutions, providing funds through over-collateralized loans while counterparties assume the risk associated with trading platforms.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic in Talks to Secure $200 Million Investment from New Private Equity Fund
data.description: Anthropic is reportedly in discussions to secure a $200 million investment from a new private equity fund. According to Jin10, the negotiations are aimed at bolstering Anthropic's financial resources to further its AI development initiatives. The Wall Street Journal reports that this potential investment reflects growing interest in AI technologies and their applications across various industries. Anthropic, known for its advancements in AI, is seeking to expand its capabilities and market presence through strategic financial partnerships. The outcome of these talks could significantly impact the company's future growth and innovation strategies.

data.title: NBA Regular Season: Trail Blazers vs. Nuggets Prediction Market Activity
data.description:
A significant transaction has been observed in the Polymarket prediction market regarding the NBA regular season game between the Portland Trail Blazers and the Denver Nuggets. According to Odaily, an account with nearly $4 million in profits has purchased 130,000 shares predicting the Trail Blazers will defeat the Nuggets, with an average opening price of 26.6 cents.

The game is scheduled to start at 9 a.m. UTC+8 today. As the NBA regular season nears its end, the Trail Blazers hold a record of 40 wins and 38 losses, placing them ninth in the Western Conference. Meanwhile, the Nuggets have a record of 50 wins and 28 losses, ranking fourth in the conference.

data.title: SEC Chairman: The Crypto Safe Harbor proposal has been submitted to the White House for review and would allow crypto projects to raise funds without immediate registration.
data.description: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins stated that the crypto "safe harbor" proposal has entered the White House review stage, and formal rules are expected to be released soon. The proposal includes a "startup exemption" mechanism, allowing crypto projects to raise funds without immediately completing registration.

data.title: Argentine president's claim of no connection to LIBRA project has been questioned after reports surfaced of phone conversations with project staff.
data.description: Argentine President Miguel Millais has seven recorded phone calls with businessman Mauricio Novelli on the evening of February 14, 2025, the launch of the LIBRA project. These calls spanned the period before and after Novelli posted promotional content on the X platform. The report also states that the latest phone evidence includes recordings of what appear to be payments and draft documents outlining financial arrangements, further casting doubt on his previous statements that he had no connection to the project. Millais has not yet been formally charged, but he has become a key target of the investigation. (The New York Times)

data.title: Anthropic plans to invest $200 million in a new private equity project.
data.description: Anthropic is planning to invest approximately $200 million in a new private equity partnership aimed at providing AI tools to its portfolio companies. Sources familiar with the matter said that institutions such as General Atlantic, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman are considering participating in the project, with an overall funding target of approximately $1 billion. This move is seen as a key step in Anthropic's expansion into enterprise-level AI services. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: Pentagon Cancels Scheduled Press Conference Without Explanation
data.description: The Pentagon has unexpectedly canceled a scheduled press conference that was set for Tuesday morning at 8 a.m. local time. According to BlockBeats, no reason was provided for the cancellation.

data.title: High Stakes in NCAA Final as Michigan Faces Connecticut
data.description:
A significant bet has been placed on the outcome of the NCAA final between Michigan and Connecticut. According to Odaily, an account with a 65.5% success rate has invested $980,000 on Michigan's victory over Connecticut, with an average opening price of 74¢.

The NCAA final is set to begin today at 8:50 PM UTC+8. Michigan, the top seed from the Big Ten Conference, advanced to the final after a decisive win over Arizona in the semifinals. Connecticut, the top seed from the Big East Conference, has reached the NCAA final three times in the past four years, winning twice, and is aiming for a third championship in four years.

Currently, on Polymarket, the probability of Michigan winning stands at 74%, while Connecticut's chances are at 27%.

data.title: Saudi Arabia’s large petrochemical facilities reportedly suffered widespread attacks
data.description: Early on the morning of the 7th local time, Iranian sources, citing unnamed sources, reported that an explosion occurred in the Jubail industrial zone in northeastern Saudi Arabia, which has US investment, and that the attack was widespread. The Jubail industrial zone is reportedly one of the world's major petrochemical production bases, with an annual output of approximately 60 million tons of petrochemical products, accounting for 6% to 8% of global production. Several large petrochemical companies and projects are located in the zone. Saudi Basic Industries Corporation (SAB) is one of the major investors in the industrial zone. In addition, the Sadara project, in which Dow Chemical Company is involved, and a project jointly invested by Saudi Aramco and Total Energy are also located in the industrial zone. (CCTV)

data.title: The Blockchain Association opposes Citadel's regulatory proposals for DeFi and supports the SEC's innovation exemption framework.
data.description: The Blockchain Association has submitted a comment letter to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in response to Citadel's previous calls for stronger regulation of DeFi. The Blockchain Association stated that DeFi protocol developers are not brokers or exchange operators and therefore cannot be included in the regulatory framework designed for traditional financial intermediaries. The organization also called on the SEC to advance an "innovation exemption" mechanism to provide regulatory pilot space for on-chain assets and tokenized securities, pointing out that comprehensive rule-making processes are lengthy and could delay technology adoption and encourage the outflow of innovation. Previously, Citadel had recommended that the SEC strengthen its regulation of DeFi through formal legislative processes. (The Block)

data.title: An account that had lost nearly $5 million purchased $400,000 worth of NCAA Major Madness Finals units, with Connecticut defeating Michigan by a point spread.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in the Polymarket "NCAA Finals: Connecticut vs. Michigan" prediction event, an account that lost nearly $5 million (address: 0x492442eab586f242b53bda933fd5de859c8a3782) purchased $400,000 worth of bets on Connecticut to win by a 6.5-point spread against Michigan, with an average opening price of 49¢. NCAA "March Madness" refers to the national championship of the NCAA Division I men's basketball league. The tournament typically begins in March each year, uses a single-elimination format, and is known for its intense competition and packed schedule. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are priced in.

data.title: Polymarket Sees Significant Bet on NCAA March Madness Outcome
data.description:
A notable transaction has occurred in the Polymarket prediction market concerning the NCAA March Madness event. According to Odaily, an account with a significant loss of nearly $5 million has invested $400,000 on Connecticut to win against Michigan with a 6.5-point spread, at an average opening price of 49 cents.

The NCAA March Madness refers to the national championship of the NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament. This event typically begins in March, featuring a single-elimination format that is known for its intense competition and tightly packed schedule.

data.title: Explosion Reported in Saudi Arabia's Jubail Industrial City
data.description:
An explosion occurred in Saudi Arabia's Jubail Industrial City, according to reports from unnamed sources on April 7. According to BlockBeats, the incident took place in the early hours of the day and involved U.S. capital participation. Jubail Industrial City is a significant global petrochemical production hub, with an annual output of approximately 60 million tons, accounting for 6% to 8% of the world's total production. The area hosts numerous large petrochemical enterprises and projects, with the Saudi Basic Industries Corporation being one of the primary investors.

Additionally, the Sadara project, involving the U.S. Dow Chemical Company, and a joint venture between Saudi Aramco and France's TotalEnergies, are also located within the industrial city.
